content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 3, 1987


Honorable Bill Bradley
Assemblyman, 76th District
State Capitol
Sacramento, CA  95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-87-020

Dear Assemblyman Bradley:

You have requested advice regarding your duties under the
conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the "Act").1/

QUESTION

Are you required to disqualify yourself from participating
in decisions involving AB 60 (Elder) of 1987?

CONCLUSION

You are not required by the Act to disqualify yourself from
participating in decisions on AB 60.

FACTS

You are a member of the California State Assembly.  You are
over 55 years of age and considering selling your present home.

AB 60 provides that a property owner over 55 years of age
may keep the current property tax assessment on his or her home
if it is sold and a new home in the same county of equal or
lesser value is purchased.


1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
indicated.  Commission regulations appear at 2 California
Administrative Code Section 18000, et seq.  All references to
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California
Administrative Code.


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916)322-5660

Honorable Bill Bradley
February 3, 1987
Page 2


ANALYSIS

Section 87100 prohibits a public official from making, participating in, or attempting to influence a governmental decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a financial interest. Section 87100 applies to state legislators, who are public officials within the meaning of Section 87100.2/ (Sections 82048, 82049.)

A public official has a financial interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on the official or a member of his or her immediate family or on, among other things:

(b) Any real property in which the public official has a direct or indirect interest worth one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.

Section 87103(b).

Thus, you are required to disqualify yourself from participating in a particular decision if the decision would have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on your residence, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally. Disqualification would be required even if you would not personally benefit from the transaction.

Regulation 18703 (copy enclosed) provides that a material financial effect on an official's interest is distinguishable from its effect on the public generally unless the decision will affect the official's interest in substantially the same manner as it will affect all members of the public or a significant segment of the public. The population of property owners over 55 years of age, who would be affected by AB 60, represents a significant segment of the public.

The particular facts of each decision you face will be determinative of the disqualification issue. However, you are not required to disqualify yourself unless the particular decision you face will affect you or a segment of the

2/ However, the remedies provided in the Act for violations of Section 87100 are not applicable to elected state officers, including state legislators. (Section 87102.)

Honorable Bill Bradley
February 3, 1987
Page 3


population smaller than the population of property owners over
55 years of age differently than other members of the public.
If AB 60 is amended in any significant respect, you would be
wise to contact us again for advice.

     If you have any questions concerning this letter, you may
contact me at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]

                                        Diane M. Griffiths
                                        General Counsel

DMG:plh
Enclosure

CAPITOL OFFICE
STATE CAPITOL
PHONE (916) 445-8521

Assembly
California Legislature

SAN DIEGO COUNTY
OFFICE
125 WEST WASHINGTON AVENUE
SUITE 105
ESCONDIDO, CA 92025
PHONE: (619) 489-8924
(619) 237-6459

BILL BRADLEY
ASSEMBLYMAN, SEVENTY-SIXTH DISTRICT
MEMBER OF THE ASSEMBLY RURAL CAUCUS

VIRGINIA RASMUSSEN
ADMINISTRATIVE ASSISTANT

ROSALIA AILANO
FIELD REPRESENTATIVE

JAN 7 1986 10 AM

RIVERSIDE COUNTY
DISTRICT OFFICE
42' 600 BOB HOPE DRIVE
SUITE 406
RANCHO MIRAGE, CA 92270
PHONE: (619) 568-0357

SHIRLEY VILE
FIELD REPRESENTATIVE

January 5, 1986


John Larsen, Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Mr. Larsen:

With the passage of Proposition 60 on the ballot in November,
legislation has been introduced providing that a property owner
over 55 years of age may keep the same property tax assessment
if his or her new home in the same county is of equal or lesser
value that the home being sold. The bill number is AB 60.

My question is, would it be a conflict of interest for me to
vote for this legislation since I am a legislator in that age
bracket and am interested in selling my present home.

Please advise.

Sincerely,

Bill Bradley

BILL BRADLEY
Assemblyman, 76th District

BE:bd

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

January 9, 1987

Honorable Bill Bradley
Assemblyman, 76th District
State Capitol
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: 87-020

Dear Assemblyman Bradley:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on January 7, 1987 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days. You also should be aware that your
letter and our response are public records which may be
disclosed to the public upon receipt of a proper request for
disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

DMG:plh

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

in decisions involving AB 60 (Elder) of 1987?

CONCLUSION: You are not required by the Act to disqualify yourself from 
participating in decisions on AB 60.
